issue_title: Problems with binary incompatibility when combining multiple channels
issue_body:
This issue recently arised in the bioconda channel (pull request bioconda/bioconda-recipes#2569). To streamline the discussion, I thought I'd move it to an appropriate place.

# Problem
When combining multiple channels that are not disjoint in terms of packages, it can happen that conda pulls, even pinned, dependencies from a different channel than the one used for building. This can lead to problems when binary incompatibilities occur that are not reflected in the package versions (e.g. different compiler flags).

# Example
The bioconda channel depends on the anaconda, r and conda-forge channels. Both conda-forge and r use only anaconda during building. Now, there is a libgd version in anaconda and one in conda-forge. During dependency resolution, it can happen that e.g. the one from conda-forge is pulled for a package from the r channel (which was however built against the anaconda version). Apparently, the two libgd versions are not binary compatible, leading to errors (see pull request bioconda/bioconda-recipes#2569).

# Possible solutions

## Channel dependencies
Make conda aware of channel dependencies. A package coming from a certain channel may only pull dependencies from the same channel or channels it was built against. The downside is that people cannot use channels to replace a certain package in a dependency tree.

## Pinning beyond plain versions
Find a way to pin to something like used compiler flags or an md5 hash of a binary. Downside: this would probably involve a lot of changes in the way conda discovers and manages package versions.

## Ensure binary compatibility case-by-case
Have an agreement between conda-forge, r and anaconda about using the same compiler flags for duplicate packages.

----

This is a pressing issue for us, and I believe this will sooner or later also occur to the users of other channels. Any help, pointers and opinions are much appreciated!

ping @croth1, @bgruening, @daler, @jakirkham, @mingwandroid, @msarahan, @csoja, @teoliphant and @pelson.

issue_title: Command paths should be case insensitive
issue_body:
Example. This works:
  az network dns record-set a show
This fails:
  az network dns record-set A show
That's strange, since DNS record types are normally capitalized!
The record type, in all DNS commands, should be case-insensitive.

issue_title: [DNS] Standardize parameter names for record data
issue_body:
There are lots of different record types in DNS, each with their own record data. That yields lots of different parameters, such as -ipv4-Address (for A records), --exchange (for MX records), etc.

In most cases, these parameter names are good. In a couple of cases however I'd like to change them to bring them into line with what we use in PowerShell and the REST API.

In addition, since adding/removing records is a *core* use case, I'd like to add single-letter shortcuts for each of these parameter names

Please see table below for suggested values (in each case, the parameter name should be an exact match to the property name passed via JSON. Using consistent names makes a consistent mental model for the customer, and makes documentation simpler, etc.)

Record type|Current Parameter|Renamed Parameter|Shortcut
---|---|---|---
A|--ipv4-address|no change|-a
AAAA|--ipv6-address|no change|-a
CNAME|--cname|no change|-c
MX|--exchange|no change|-e
MX|--preference|no change|-p
NS|--dname|**--nsdname**|-d
PTR|--dname|**--ptrdname**|-d
SOA|--email|no change|-e
SOA|--expire-time|no change|-x
SOA|--minimum-ttl|no change|-m
SOA|--refresh-time|no change|-f
SOA|--retry-time|no change|-r
SOA|--serial-number|no change|-s
SRV|--priority|no change|-p
SRV|--weight|no change|-w
SRV|--port|no change|-r
SRV|--target|no change|-t
TXT|--value|no change|-v

issue_title: Build a release life cycle more reliable
issue_body:
Hi @jettify sorry the channel used to approx you, I didn't know how to do it.

Right now the unique serious attempt to have `botocore` working in an asynchronous environment is this project, unfortunately IMHO there is still work to do to give a full asynchronous package working for all of the AWS services. I would like to help in the direction to have this package in the best position to make it an alternative to the official synchronous SDK, how?

1) Functional tests

**Botocore** has a set of end to end tests that check different execution paths from the client function to a mocked version of the HTTP response. This ensures that all of the glue between the client and the network layer works properly. 

I will propose to "copy" all functional tests from the **botcore** repository using **aiobotcore**, this will help this package in three different things:

- Increase the coverage making sure that the **aiobotcore** substitution does not break the glue and the end to end tests work properly.
- Raise unnoticed bugs
- Build a methodology that allows **aiobotcore** to relay in something at each new **botcore** release

2) Integration tests

The same as the functional ones, but in this case having the issue to the need of have a real AWS account. Here, most of the tests end up hitting real AWS resources. Here I can try to involve my organization to allocate this resources. However, I would like to know how the people from **botcore** is doing this process in their releases, taking into account that these tests are not part of the public CI /cc @jamesls
 
This also comes with the same benefits already commented in the previous point.

3) Version alignment

Why not go for an alignment of the versions between **botcore** and **aiobotcore** ? This will help at least in the following things:

- A cycle of releases based on publishes a new **aiobotcore** when a new **botcore** version appears. That would imply among other things update the functional and integration tests, relaying on the tests results to make finally the aligned release.
- Make the life easier for the developers.

4) Move the current test suite to something like *ut*

... and remove those ones that are in fact a duplication of the integrations/functional that come from **botocore**

IMHO these four things might help this project to get more velocity, and the most important **have a solid foundation to make the release life cycle more trustable**. I know that this is work, and in some way can be considered a tedious work. Regarding this, I could try to organize a hackathon to make it possible.

issue_title: Getting `TypeError: float() argument must be a string or a number`
issue_body:
First of all, great library! I'm finding it very useful for some projects that I am working on.
However, in some instances I am running into a TypeError in models where an array is being sliced or assigned as a block within a larger array (despite being scalar functions ultimately). 

I can reproduce it with a stripped-down example:

https://gist.github.com/thearn/faba933208316d71cdb9

```python

import autograd.numpy as np
from autograd import grad

def f(A):

    B = np.zeros((4,4))

    B[:2, :2] = A

    return B.sum()

A = np.random.randn(2,2)

df = grad(f)

print df(A)
```

which gives the traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/tristanhearn/Dropbox/code/adcomponent/src/adcomponent/test.py", line 17, in <module>
    print df(A)
  File "/Users/tristanhearn/Documents/thearn_repos/autograd/autograd/core.py", line 20, in gradfun
    return backward_pass(*forward_pass(fun,args,kwargs,argnum))
  File "/Users/tristanhearn/Documents/thearn_repos/autograd/autograd/core.py", line 61, in forward_pass
    end_node = fun(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/tristanhearn/Dropbox/code/adcomponent/src/adcomponent/test.py", line 9, in f
    B[:2, :2] = A
TypeError: float() argument must be a string or a number
```

Is this expected (ie. a known and accepted limitation)?

issue_title: Some examples are not working with python 3
issue_body:
For example I got the following error for `neural_net_regression.py`

```
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'outputs' referenced before assignment
```

and the following error for `convnet.py`

```
AttributeError: module 'urllib' has no attribute 'urlretrieve'
```

Ndarrays containing 32 bit floats have half the memory footprint of 64 bit, and operations on them are also significantly faster (I expect this is different on different systems but the speedup seems to be around 8x for basic operations on my computer, using numpy with OpenBLAS). At the moment we have
```python
>>> import autograd
>>> import autograd.numpy as np
>>> 
>>> def f(x):
...     return np.sum(x**2)
... 
>>> # Array y with dtype np.float32
>>> y = np.random.randn(10, 10).astype(np.float32)
>>> 
>>> autograd.grad(f)(y).dtype
dtype('float64')
```

~~I'm pretty sure this is because the `1.0` value on [this line]( https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/core.py#L49) defaults to a float64, so it should be pretty easy to properly support `float32` by testing the type of the value of the endnode and copying that type.~~

issue_title: Pulsar loses jobs if restarted during job setup

issue_body:
Especially `shed_lint --urls` could seriously benefit from a task queue and a number of worker threads popping off that queue.

I imagine we could apply that same theory to a number of other similarly recursive operations where we're doing one "thing" per discovered repository.

- [ ] Suppress extra output when running ``--conformance-test``.
- [ ] "Stage" files out of Galaxy.
- [ ] Implement additional options available to reference tool.
  - [ ] Conditional Docker support.
- [ ] Allow running the reference implementation this way.

One of the prerequisites for implementing multi-reader storage is to ensure that Alice can discover all of the public keys needed to authenticate data written by Bob in application `foo.com`.  This is achieved with a "key file," whose tentative structure is documented [here](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-browser/blob/master/docs/key-delegation.md).

In this design, there is one major drawback of key files: all software that expects to receive a profile will break.  This is because the key file specification moves the profile inside the key file.

I think this can be solved simply by changing the structure of the key file to be a well-formed profile, but with some extra information.  The signature requirements on app keys, delegation keys, and profile data still hold, but the verification steps will change to the following:

1. get name owner address
2. get name zonefile
3. get name profile
4. extract app keys and delegation keys
5. verify that the public keys in the delegation object JWT match its signature
6. verify that the name owner address was derived from the delegation object's public keys (i.e. the delegation object's public keys correspond to the private keys that own the name)
7. verify that each app key JWTs was signed by the appropriate device's delegation public key
8. verify that the profile was signed by one of the delegation object's public keys

This would be just as secure as what was originally proposed, but with the added benefit that **all resolvers and explorers will keep on working without modification.**  The only remaining engineering tasks would be to:

[ ] finalize the app key derivation logic (blockstack-core, blockstack-browser)
[ ] automatically generate and insert the app and delegation JWTs into a profile when the owner starts the browser (blockstack-browser)
[ ] derive and insert application- and device-specific keys into the app key JWT when the user signs into an app for the first time (blockstack-core, blockstack-browser)
[ ] invalidate any existing application sessions to force upgrading to multi-reader Gaia (blockstack-core)

cc @kantai @shea256 @larrysalibra

issue_title: Damaged screenshot files saved in HTML report assets folder on OSX
issue_body:
The PNG files that pytest-selenium writes to the "assets" folder that it creates in support of its HTML report cannot be opened on OSX 10.11.6. This is pytest-selenium 1.3.1 with selenium 2.53.6 as installed via pip, running under Python 3.5.1 and using --driver Firefox.

The images are displayed as broken image icons in the report, and attempts to open the PNG files directly produce "The file (long, randomized name).png could not be opened … it may be damaged or use a file format that Preview doesn't recognize" from Preview. The image files are not empty; one of them is 111 KB in size and the other is 3.8 MB.

When I use the WebDriver's save_screenshot method (from the "selenium" fixture) directly at the end of my tests, it saves undamaged and correct screenshot PNG images (36 KB and 1.3 MB). In this case, the tests are successful, while I intentionally made tests fail in order to produce screenshots in pytest-selenium's HTML report.

        key_set = set(kwargs.keys())

issue_title: System.Data.SqlClient throwing System.PlatformNotSupportedException when loaded via reflection
issue_body:
#  System.Data.SqlClient throwing System.PlatformNotSupportedException when loaded via reflection

System.Data.SqlClient does not work when loading the external library (.NET Standard DLL) at runtime through reflection except if the library has been published for a specific platform (rid).
Trying to execute a SQL query against a SQL Server instance throws "System.PlatformNotSupportedException: System.Data.SqlClient is not supported on this platform" exception.

Repro steps and Visual studio solution can be found here:
https://github.com/softeering/NETStandardSQLClientRepro

I'm pretty sure this can be explained but I don't get why it doesn't work

diff --git a/dateutil/rrule.py b/dateutil/rrule.py

                     dtstart = parser.parse(value, ignoretz=ignoretz,

issue_title: Evaluate use of libuv-cffi bindings as a replacement for PyUV
issue_body:
I was just looking into using PyPy as the Python interpreter for Powerline, but PyUV cannot be built for PyPy (see saghul/pyuv#49). There exist, however, [cffi bindings for libuv](https://github.com/koehlma/uv), which also work with PyPy (see the [feature list](https://github.com/koehlma/uv#features)).

It would be nice to see whether these could be used as a replacement for PyUV on systems where they are present and fallback to PyUV on systems where they are not. According to the author of the bindings, @koehlma, "it is not fully pyuv compatible, although it should be possible to write a thin pyuv compatibility layer".

The Haurwitz model takes apparent_zenith as its argument.  pvlib ephemeris calculations can return zenith>90 for times after sunset. The Haurwitz model includes this term: 
`np.exp(-0.059/cos_zenith)`
which causes a numerical overflow when cos_zenith is small and negative, i.e., apparent_zenith is greater than but close to 90.
The current Haurwitz model code obscures the warning by 
` clearsky_ghi[clearsky_ghi < 0] = 0`
`clearsky_ghi < 0` occurs whenever `cos_zenith<0`
I propose that we apply the Haurwitz formula only for `cos_zenith>0` and set `clearsky_ghi=0` otherwise.
The function output would remain unchanged but the overflow condition would be handled.

issue_body:
Hi,

I'm not sure if that was already covered in an other issue (couldn't find it) or intentional in the code, but when using the function ``get_solarposition()`` with ``method='nrel_c''', it doesn't seem to matter whether the time index is localized or not.
The function will assume that the time index is at 'UTC' no matter what. Which goes against what was described in http://pvlib-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/timetimezones.html.

So I ended up converting my timezone to 'UTC' before calculation, and then back to local after the calculation

Example:
``pvlib_loc = location.Location(latitude, longitude, tz='Etc/GMT+8')
localized_index = index.tz_localize('Etc/GMT+8')
utc_index = localized_index.tz_convert('UTC')
solarAngles = pvlib_loc.get_solarposition(utc_index, method='nrel_c')
solarAngles.index = localized_index``

issue_title: Confusing validation error when submitting JSON in Django REST API format
issue_body:
Thanks for creating this project! I really like both Django REST Framework and the design of jsonapi.org, so being able to use them together is excellent.

However, with the current 2.0.0a2 release, I found an interesting problem with the interaction between DRFJA's validation errors, and DRF's presentation of those errors through the browsable API: https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/issues/3446

What appears to be happening is that when a create request is submitted using DRF's default format (i.e. an attribute mapping at the top level of the JSON request), DRFJA is raising ValidationError with the details attribute set to a list, which DRF then attempts to treat as a mapping.

Assuming I've identified the origins of the error correctly, If the exception details stored a mapping instead, that could both report the field name that was expected, as well as avoid the secondary failure in DRF's error handling.

issue_title: Viewset list when using polymorphic models
issue_body:
I have been trying to get the support for polymorphic model serialization to work in my latest project. When I request a specific object from my endpoint, the correct serializer is clearly instantiated and I receive the output that I would expect. When I try and retrieve a list of model instances, however, the following exception is raised:

```

Request Method: GET
Request URL: http://localhost:8000/projects
Django Version: 1.11
Exception Type: AttributeError
Exception Value: 'list' object has no attribute '_meta'
Exception Location: django-rest-framework-json-api/rest_framework_json_api/serializers.py in get_polymorphic_serializer_for_instance, line 279
```

I have tried running the example project, provided in the repo, and I'm able to reproduce the error I have been seeing.

Looking in the documentation, it is not clear whether there is currently support for serializing multiple model instances in a viewset list output. Looking at the tests for the project, I also do not see a test for this scenario.

Is this something that should be supported?

django-rest-framework-json-api v2.0.0-beta.2
I have defined `resource_name` attribute into Meta class of my serializer:
```python 
class MySerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    email = serializers.EmailField(required=True)
    class Meta:
        resource_name = 'user'
```
In this case the `get_resource_type_from_serializer` will always raise AttributeError, because there isn't defined the `model` attribute.
Code of `get_resource_type_from_serializer`:
```python
def get_resource_type_from_serializer(serializer):
    return getattr(
        serializer.Meta,
        'resource_name',
        get_resource_type_from_model(serializer.Meta.model))
```

issue_title: Mutagen writes, but won't read, multiple identical front cover images in MP3 files
issue_body:
Mutagen allowed me to write two identical front cover images, so now the files have two identical APIC tags. However, mutagen will only read one of them, nor will it tell me there are multiple front cover images so I could handle the issue.  mid3v2 only lists one APIC tag as well, while id3v2 lists both tags, and easyTAG also shows both front cover images.


`$ mid3v2 -l ../music/mp3/Queen/Greatest\ Hits\ I\ \&\ II/Queen\ -\ Under\ Pressure.mp3 
IDv2 tag info for ../music/mp3/Queen/Greatest Hits I & II/Queen - Under Pressure.mp3
APIC=cover front, Front cover (image/jpeg, 65082 bytes)
COMM=description=XXX=<rating>6</rating>
TALB=Greatest Hits I & II
`

`$ id3v2 -l ../music/mp3/Queen/Greatest\ Hits\ I\ \&\ II/Queen\ -\ Under\ Pressure.mp3 
id3v2 tag info for ../music/mp3/Queen/Greatest Hits I & II/Queen - Under Pressure.mp3:
TCON (Content type): Classic Rock (1)
APIC (Attached picture): (Front cover)[, 3]: image/jpeg, 65082 bytes
APIC (Attached picture): (Front cover)[, 3]: image/jpeg, 65082 bytes
COMM (Comments): (description)[XXX]: <rating>6</rating>
`

Both
`            d = mutagen.mp3.MP3(self.mp3Path)
            x = d.tags.getall('APIC')`
and
`self.apic = self.m.getall('APIC')`
where m is: `self.m = mutagen.id3.ID3(self.mp3Path, None, False, 3)`
only return one image, when a list of apic objects is expected.

It'd be nice if mutagen didn't write off-spec files, and it'd also be nice if mutagen would read off-spec apic tags so you could detect the off-spec condition and fix it.

The following bit of code, combined with partial dependency on pathlib2 looks weird/suspicious:

```python
try:
    from pathlib import Path
except ImportError:
    # Python 2 backport
    from pathlib2 import Path
```

I'd like to note that — besides the stdlib [pathlib module](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html) that is present since Python 3.4, there are two backports out there: [pathlib](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pathlib) and [pathlib2](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pathlib2).

The pathlib backport aims to backport the basic pathlib module (from Python 3.4, I think) to older versions of Python in a forward-compatible way. Since it uses the same name as the standard module, it is impossible to use it in Python 3.4+ (the standard module shadows it).

The pathlib2 package is a rolling backport of pathlib from the newest version of Python. It uses a different module name, so that it can be used in newer versions of Python as well (possibly providing newer functions that the pathlib module from the particular version of Python).

That considered, I see two possibilities:
1. you need pathlib with Python 3.4 API — in which case you just `inherit pathlib` unconditionally, and add a dependency on `pathlib` package in Python < 3.4;
2. you need pathlib with newer API — in which case you need to `inherit pathlib2` also in some newer versions of Python, and add appropriate dependency on `pathlib2`.

I think pickleshare falls into the first category. In which case the current code is incorrect since when `pathlib` is installed, then `pathlib2` is never used and the dependency is unnecessary.

This comes from the work I'm doing in PR #1289

If I have a bit-vector that should be interpreted as a signed quantity, how should I communicate that to the call in `maximize/minimize`?

If there are operators used around, then z3 seems to be able to "infer" the signedness (though I think this may not be enough either.) But in the extreme case when there is nothing but the type, I'm not sure how to communicate this properly.

As a concrete input, consider this:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_BV)
(declare-fun s0 () (_ BitVec 8))
(maximize s0)
(check-sat)
(get-objectives)
(get-value (s0))
```

This rightfully produces:

```
sat
(objectives
 (s0 255)
)
((s0 #xff))
```

But what should I do to tell the solver that `s0` is actually to be interpreted as a signed quantity, and hence the maximum value it can actually take is `127`?

It almost looks like we need `umaximize`/`smaximize`/`uminimize`/`sminimize` to communicate the intent, following the similar operators in the bit-vector theory. Or, is there a trick I can use to make sure this is communicated properly?

Thanks!

    (set-option :fixedpoint.engine datalog)

    (declare-rel r0 ())

issue_title: Make it easier to discover kernels to install
issue_body:
When a user opens [nteract for the first time](https://twitter.com/denormalize/status/824472075230343168) (or any time really), they need a way to discover other kernels. I think there are multiple opportunities:

* Include a menu item for discovering new kernels (could link to a new page)
* Create our own editorial version of [Jupyter kernels](https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter/wiki/Jupyter-kernels) that lists kernels we support well (and welcome contributions to it).

issue_title: Make Travis happier to work with
issue_body: I'm not sure what limits we're running into right now (disk space, memory?). Travis seems to be stalling out on the regular while I'm able to build and run this all locally on Linux and OS X.

We need to really double down on how we view the current kernel status:

* If we haven't gotten a message back from the kernel in a long time, we should ask for status and declare it as disconnected in the UI
* Any kernel process logs should end up in a "log drawer", similar to play
* If the internet goes out (within any of the web apps), we should evaluate if the kernel is still connected and display something about being "offline"


When the kernel is starting, we need to show:

* when the kernel is being launched
* any other states

issue_title: pip installing your sdk raises a module not found error
issue_body:
Pip uses your setup.py file to find and install dependencies, however in your setup.py file on line 20 you import your exponent_server_sdk package which in turn imports six in it's __init__.py file. Pip hasn't gotten around to installing six yet, so this breaks.

I'm sure your oversight is only mildly irritating for most use cases, because you just manually install your dependencies first. If one's app sits in a docker container however, then it becomes moderately irritating to make sure that your dependencies installed before everything else because those are spun up from scratch with no manual intervention.

This is a test plan for issue: https://github.com/pulp/pulp/pull/2920

## Ensure Status API filters out old records

1. With a normal Pulp 2.13+ running
2. Capture the `pulp-admin status` output to show that all processes are shown correctly. You can also do this with the API.
3. Kill celerybeat. Do not stop it gracefully. I do this using:  `sudo pkill -9 -f 'celery beat'`. If you stop celerybeat gracefully this test is meaningless.
4. Immediately after killing celerybeat show the `pulp-admin status` and verify that the `scheduler@` entry is shown.
5. Wait 30 seconds
6. Show the `pulp-admin status` again or use the API
7. Verify that the `scheduler@` entry is missing

## Ensure the Resource Manager filter out old records

1. Stop your Pulp 2.13+ system completely
2. Take your system down to 1 worker by editing `/etc/default/pulp_workers` to set `PULP_CONCURRENCY=1`
3. Start all pulp services
4. Verify that only 1 worker is running `ps -awfux | grep reserved_resource`. Here is my output:

```
[vagrant@dev ~]$ ps -awfux | grep reserved_resource
vagrant   9681  0.0  0.0  12740   996 pts/2    S+   21:50   0:00                          \_ grep --color=auto reserved_resource
apache    9362  4.8  3.8 590328 79572 ?        Ssl  21:49   0:01 /bin/python /usr/bin/celery worker -n reserved_resource_worker-0@%h -A pulp.server.async.app -c 1 --events --umask 18 --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/reserved_resource_worker-0.pid --heartbeat-interval=5
apache    9530  0.0  2.9 511684 60952 ?        S    21:49   0:00  \_ /bin/python /usr/bin/celery worker -n reserved_resource_worker-0@%h -A pulp.server.async.app -c 1 --events --umask 18 --pidfile=/var/run/pulp/reserved_resource_worker-0.pid --heartbeat-interval=5
```

5. Kill the only worker. Do not gracefully stop it. I do this with: `sudo pkill -9 -f reserved_resource`
6. Verify the worker is stopped with `ps -awfux | grep reserved_resource`
7. Wait 30 seconds
8. Sync a zoo repo using `pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id zoo`
9. Verify that the task stays in the Waiting state and never begins

issue_title: Test migration for transition from basic to advanced modular support
issue_body:
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3659

1. with pulp 2.16 create and sync modular repo
2. upgrade to 2.17, run migration
3. ensure yum_repo_metadata_file which contained modules.yaml was removed from the repo
4. instead modulemd and modulemd_defaults content were identified and associated to the repo
5. list of repositories to re-publish was provided (by migration)

I'm not entirely sure, but I believe that we test the pulp_python plugin as though it were version 2.0 if the platform version is 2.12.2 or greater. There was a bit of confusion around exactly what version of Pulp would ship with pulp_python 2.0 that was resolved recently. Now, we're going to be releasing pulp_python 2.0 with Pulp 2.13.0.

I think this means that selectors [like this](https://github.com/PulpQE/pulp-smash/blob/master/pulp_smash/tests/python/api_v2/test_sync_publish.py#L115) (where both a bug is checked for testability and a version is checked) should be updated to not be tested unless the Pulp under test is 2.13 or greater. The bugs related to these new python features will be returning to a testable state shortly and will likely cause false negative results in runs on 2.12.z, but should succeed on builds for Pulp 2.13:

- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/135
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/136
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/140
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1882

Create repo a repo
$pulp-admin rpm repo create --repo-id=repo_1

Upload lanpacks

$ pulp-admin rpm repo uploads langpacks -i hyphen -n hyphen-%s --repo-id repo_1

List the repo
$ pulp-admin repo list --repo-id repo_1

+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
                              Repositories
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Id:                   repo_1
Display Name:         None
Description:          None
Content Unit Counts:
  Package Langpacks: 1

Remove langpacks
$ pulp-admin rpm repo remove langpacks --repo-id repo_1 --str-eq repo_id=repo_1

$ pulp-admin repo list --repo-id repo_1

+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
                              Repositories
+----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Id:                   repo_1
Display Name:         None
Description:          None
Content Unit Counts:
  Package Langpacks: 0

issue_title: Getting information from info doesn't seem to work anymore
issue_body:
Hey guys, 

I was trying to train SpaceInvaders by myself and got the following error message
`python -m baselines.deepq.experiments.atari.train --env SpaceInvaders`

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/runpy.py", line 170, in _run_module_as_main
    "__main__", mod_spec)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/runpy.py", line 85, in _run_code
    exec(code, run_globals)
  File "/home/username/baselines/baselines/baselines/deepq/experiments/atari/train.py", line 244, in <module>
    start_time, start_steps = time.time(), info['steps']
KeyError: 'steps'
```

Is it not possible anymore to access `info` like this? If so, it should be considered rewriting it. 
Has anybody else encountered this problem?

I'm getting this error while trying to install it in python 3.6.3 - 64 bits on windows, I tried installing it by using pip3 install --upgrade baselines, the method on READ ME and pip install git+https://github.com/openai/baselines 

all of then led to the same error ```Failed building wheel for atari-py```
Any way to solve this?

Hi,

In the C code of mujoco, we could set the state of the robot by: `mju_copy(data->qpos, ptr, model->nq)`, where ptr is the pointer to the data to be copied. But in python, it doesn't allow me to copy any value to the `model.data.qpos`.

JSON doesn't have a date format so dates in graphene are just string fields. This means any dates that are output without being formatted first (e.g. if you use a DjangoNode with only_fields where one of the fields is a date field) are just output as `unicode(date_object)` (which looks like `2016-03-29 17:42:01.683650+00:00`). If you take that date in JavaScript and do `new Date(myDateStr)` browsers will probably work it out but it's not an officially supported date format (as we found out when we tried to parse it in React Native - i.e. Javascript Core on iOS - which doesn't handle it).

I'm happy to create a pull request but I'm not sure what the best solution would be. Here are some options:
 * Make all dates output as `2016-10-01T14:12:00+01:00` which is a format JavaScript understands (which makes sense since we're outputting JSON)
     * if we go down this route I'm not sure where the best place to hook it in would be - maybe another special case in coerce_string in graphql-core like there is for booleans?
     * another option would be to just do it for Django fields - make a DateTimeString type with a separate serialize function and modify convert_django_field for the DateField
 * Do one of the above but make it configurable somehow
 * If those are't options (e.g. for backwards compatibility) then it would be nice to be able to hook in and do it ourselves without having to think about a fix every time we encounter a date field

Happy to do the work. If you'd like that, let me know which option you'd prefer and I'll open a PR.

issue_title: Add workaround for LCOW USER bug
issue_body:
Currently the use of LCOW is blocked by a bug with docker-for-windows.  LCOW start with the user set to `root` regardless of what is set in `Dockerfile`, while CPT assumes the user is `conan` (more importantly, that `pwd` is `/home/conan`.  The suggestion here is to add `sudo su conan && ` when LCOW is detected. Currently, CPT has no concept of LCOW, so that would need to be added also.  

The issue for the user problem on LCOW is documented here:
https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/36469

There are other ways to workaround the issue, this one just seemed fine.

issue_title: Create a new assertion for filtered file comparison
issue_body:
We could use a new assertion that can use more complex filters (i.e. more than just the single-line filtering supported by assertFileEqualsBaseline).

The idea is to accept two new parameters:

    a filter function that takes a stream / string and returns filtered stream / string.
    a comparison function 

The motivating case is checking PICO results with a non-zero optimality gap: the filter would parse through the result file and identify and return the final solution, and the comparison function would implement "assertLessThan(result, baseline*(1+gap))"

issue_title: Handcrafting guide is broken while using json dumps to serialize handcrafted tx to json str
issue_body:
* bigchaindb-driver version: bigchaindb-driver==0.4.0
* Python version: Python 3.5.2
* Operating System: Ubuntu 16.04.2 LTS(Xenial)

### Description
While following the handcrafting guide for a CREATE transaction, came across an issue after creating a transaction ID right after the **Up to now** section. I was trying to serialize the **handcrafted_creation_tx** to json string as per the guide and it fails by complaining that the transaction ID is not JSON serializable. Same scenario with **prepared_creation_tx**

### What I Did

```
json_str_handcrafted_tx = json.dumps(handcrafted_creation_tx, sort_keys=True)
AND
json_str_prepared_tx = json.dumps(prepared_creation_tx, sort_keys=True)
```
###### Traceback
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/json/__init__.py", line 237, in dumps
    **kw).encode(obj)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 198, in encode
    chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 256, in iterencode
    return _iterencode(o, 0)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 179, in default
    raise TypeError(repr(o) + " is not JSON serializable")
TypeError: b'be9cd8825d6d7cf5f1ac81883e9c6b7c43f7ada92a4321c135c5ec5a2f9c637b' is not JSON serializable
```

###### Solution

1. One fix can be to use rapidjson for the aforementioned commands:
```
import rapidjson
json_str_handcrafted_tx = rapidjson.dumps(handcrafted_creation_tx, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
json_str_prepared_tx = rapidjson.dumps(prepared_creation_tx, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
```
2. Another fix can be to decode the 'id' to utf-8 before calling json.dumps
```
handcrafted_creation_tx['id'] = handcrafted_creation_tx['id'].decode('utf-8')
prepared_creation_tx['id'] = prepared_creation_tx['id'].decode('utf-8')

json_str_handcrafted_tx = json.dumps(handcrafted_creation_tx, sort_keys=True)
json_str_prepared_tx = json.dumps(prepared_creation_tx, sort_keys=True)

handcrafted_creation_tx['id'] = handcrafted_creation_tx['id'].encode('utf-8')
prepared_creation_tx['id'] = prepared_creation_tx['id'].encode('utf-8')

```

this has been reported in Debian as http://bugs.debian.org/824252

```

The -t (--tabs) and -d (--delimiter) options only change the
input delimiter, while the output delimiter is hard-coded to
be a comma.

There doesn't appear to be any way to change the output delimiter.

Using tr or similar to work around this is unreliable - there may be
actual commas in any of the tab-separated fields.  It also defeats the
purpose of using specialised tools like csvcut.

e.g. see example usage of csvcut and comments at
http://unix.stackexchange.com/a/186354/7696


The output delimiter should either be the same as the input delimiter
or there should be extra options (perhaps -T and -D) to set it
(similar to the way awk's FS and OFS variables work)
```
You provide code to go from the JWK to the key object, but not vice versa.

issue_title: Move read-only settings from cmdenvironment to set
issue_body:
The **cmdenvironment** command outputs settings that the end user can not modify. This command is generally only useful to developers, and may be confusing to users.

Suggest modifying the set command to accept a **-a/--all** option. If you do **set --all** then you get the standard settings, and then a blank line, and then a line that says "Read only settings", and then the output of cmdenvironment. That way this information would still be available, but would be less confusing for end users because there would be no cmdenvironment command in help.

issue_title: define the structure of JSON-encoded objects (for the API)
issue_body:
Related to #10 

*The structure of the JSON-encoding of objects has implications on how a client has to deserialise them again. Therefore a logical structure has to be thought of.*

**The current proposal**:

see PR [280](https://github.com/ezdac/raiden/blob/websocket_server/raiden/api/encoding.py#L51)

every object will be enveloped with information on what type of object the JSON-string represents and how to decode it (in terms of an API-version specifier):

For example an ChannelSettled - Event will look like this:

```python
 {
            'type': 'Event',
            'classifier': 'channel_settled',
            'version': '0.9b',
            'data': {
                'netting_channel_address': '0x624c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                'block_number': '1'
            },
 }
```
- the `type` specifies the Base-class of the Object `Event`, `Channel`, ...
- the `classifier` specifies the Sub-Class of the Object `channel_settled`, `channel_new`, `null`
- the `version` specifies the API-version that encoded the object
- `data` will contain the nested, encoded Object itself


There will also be a `List` type, that represents some flat list of objects, whose base-class (former: `type`) is specified in the `classifier`

```python
 {
            'type': 'List',
            'classifier': 'channel',
            'version': '0.9b',
            'data': [{
                    'netting_channel_address': '0x624c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'partner_address': '0x524c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'asset_address': '0x424c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'balance': 42,
                    'block_number': '1',
                    'status': 'closed'
                },
                {
                    'netting_channel_address': '0x324c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'partner_address': '0x724c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'asset_address': '0x425c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'balance': 42,
                    'block_number': '4',
                    'status': 'open'
                }
            ]
 }
```

Especially for events, this List has to support polymorphic fields, since there will be differently structured Events appended. Here, following the proposed schema, we would also have to envelope every Event in the list.

The proposed implementation of encoding uses for the most part the `webargs` [module](https://github.com/sloria/webargs),
which heavily wraps around the `marshmallow` [module](https://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

### To Do:
- please give some feedback on the encoding schema
- especially think about how objects should be enveloped in regard to flat lists that represent arbitrary collections of objects

### To Do *implementation wise*:
- construct a custom `marshmallow.field.Field` for polymorphic fields or use the `marshmallow-polyfield` [extension](https://github.com/Bachmann1234/marshmallow-polyfield)

issue_title: Difference of valid variable names between Matlab and Python
issue_body: In Matlab, the variable names beginning with underline(_) are not the valid variable. However, it is valid in Python. So it might cause some problems. Although it is a tiny problem, I think we should specify that in the documentation.

The batch mode of SoS makes extensive use of multiprocessing to execute steps, nested workflow etc. Because of the different process model, forking a process under windows is a very costly process. Moreever, because the forked process is new, SoS would have to copy all the data over (pickle), which is very slow. The consequence of this is that sos would run really slowly under windows.

The solution would be:

1. Use multi-process model so that sos would behave the same across platforms.
2. Disable batch mode under windows, which is ok because batch and windows do not play well anyway.
3. Use a single process for all steps, which is basically how the interactive mode is working.

I tend to use option 3 because this would make sos usable under windows with acceptable performance.

issue_title: [V3 Utils/Mod] Mod helpers allow users to be passed, raising AttributeError
issue_body:
### Type:

- Bug

### Brief description of the problem
Following #1185, `is_admin_or_superior` and `is_mod_or_superior` allow `discord.User` to be passed in, but when this occurs, `AttributeError` is raised.

**UPDATE:** these functions really shouldn't be used for `discord.User` at all, since it doesn't make any logical sense to check for permissions on a `User` object without any guild. So to resolve this issue we should really be type-checking the arguments to the function before using it, i.e. in mod.py and filter.py.

### Expected behavior
Helpers should raise `TypeError` (as it was prior to #1185)

### Actual behavior
```py
AttributeError: 'User' object has no attribute 'guild'
  File "discord/client.py", line 223, in _run_event
    yield from coro(*args, **kwargs)
  File "filter/filter.py", line 266, in on_message_edit
    mod_or_superior = await is_mod_or_superior(self.bot, obj=author)
  File "redbot/core/utils/mod.py", line 134, in is_mod_or_superior
    server = obj.guild
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Pass a `discord.User` into `is_admin_or_superior` or `is_mod_or_superior`

issue_title: Why LightPipes should be put in try finally block?
issue_body:
I found this in examples:
```python
try:
	LP=LightPipes.Init()
        ...
finally:
	del LightPipes
```

When traversing the graph looking for the proteins that encode "WB:WBGene00017137", one of the nodes returned has an identifier with the value ":.well-known/genid/NCBIGene65018product"

Looks like a parsing error to me. Plus this is the human homolog, PTEN induced putative kinase 1 [ Homo sapiens (human) ] , not the wormbase protein.

issue_title: Step time cost is very different between images on FasterRCNN example
issue_body:
When I run FasterRCNN example using HrovodTrainer, 
I find that rank step time cost are very different because of different image shape and number of obeject to detect. So the Negotiation phase in the horovod timeline are very long.

I want to konw that if this means the most time cost are in backbone which has many Conv2D 
and How would I improve the train speed 

When I export TF_CUDNN_USE_AUTOTUNE=0 as you said in  the  [link](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/13114#issuecomment-330381682) ,the step cost is still very different and no improve of performence

While writing up [insert issue # here], I was thinking about a neat feature that curio has. I'm not sure if it's very important, but I envy it a bit, so I might as well open an issue to dump thoughts out where we can see them.

The feature is: say you use `run_sync_in_worker_thread` to enter a thread, and then from the thread you use `BlockingTrioPortal.run` to re-enter trio. So conceptually, this is all one stack, that starts in trio, goes into a thread, and then goes back into trio. Now suppose the original `run_sync_in_worker_thread` is cancelled. It would be neat if this caused the code inside the `BlockingTrioPortal.run` call to raise a `Cancelled` error that propagated all the way back out of trio, through the thread, and back into trio.

See [insert issue # here] for more details about why this is tricky, and hard to fix even by extending the current cancel API.

I guess either we need some way to reenter a given cancel binding (which feels very weird and unlikely to have other use cases), or else some way to capture the exception in `run_sync_in_worker_thread` (<- this part we have) and then inject it into the eventual task (<- this part we don't have). Well... unless we do it by making a new cancel scope inside the eventual task, cancel it, and then re-raise after the inner cancellation unwinds. (We could even fudge the traceback if we want to be really tricky.) Or... we could switch from attaching `Cancelled` objects to specific scopes/bindings, and make it so that each `with` block checks if it is associated with the outermost cancelled scope, and if so it catches all `Cancelled` exceptions, and then it would be fine to manually capture a `Cancelled` from inside the re-entry task and pass it over to the thread, before it gets caught. Except, ugh, that won't work if the exception isn't `Cancelled` but rather `KeyboardInterrupt`.

It wouldn't be terrible to manually cancel the re-entry task, then call the `raise_cancel` function to get the real exception, and attach the internal exception to it as a `__context__` (though this would require #285).

Alternatively... can we avoid all this rigmorale? What if `run_sync_in_worker_thread` created a nursery, and when `BTP.run` re-entered it *put tasks into that nursery*, instead of the system nursery? Then any cancel scope that contained the `run_sync_in_worker_thread` call would also automatically contain any re-entry tasks.

`KeyboardInterrupt` might still require some careful handling, since it doesn't follow the normal cancel scope scoping rules. I guess we could do some shenanigans like pushing the actual wait-for-thread into a child task? Actually this would be pretty simple: open nursery, start a child to do the actual thread waiting stuff, then park in the nursery `__aexit__` – `KeyboardInterrupt` will be delivered to `__aexit__` and cause the nursery to be cancelled. (Actually, this is an interesting possible strategy for delivering `KeyboardInterrupt` in general: pick a nursery and inject it directly there, as if it were raised by a task inside that nursery. Or do this to the system nursery, though that feels weird.) I'd be a *little* nervous about adding overhead to `run_sync_in_worker_thread`, but maybe it wouldn't be that bad.

1) Alternative to log or sqrt for "potential_method" - a nice compromise could be the [inverse hyperbolic sine function](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/images/interactive/ArcSinhReal.gif). Continuous from neg. inf. to pos. inf. and exhibits logarithmic properties asymptotically - therefore log-scales values without need for imputation. 

    - Others in the bioinformatics field have begun using this transformation, e.g: [Michael Hoffman](https://twitter.com/michaelhoffman/status/707252800359686144) at U. Toronto. 

2) Mysterious cell in the "remove rare cells" cell - probably is just a mistake?

     - `genes_keep=sum(data)>10;  data=data(:,genes_keep); genes=genes(genes_keep)`

3) Alternative method for getting users introduced to method - have the tutorial be housed in a Google CoLab setting, i.e: https://colab.research.google.com/. This would remove any barrier to installation as a user goes through a first pass. Of course, afterwards, they would still need to install it and all that that entails. Distill.pub uses CoLab notebooks to allow interested readers to do exactly this - https://distill.pub/2018/building-blocks/.

issue_title: Listing all files from repository fails because of pagination
issue_body:
I was trying to list all files on given branch in given repository using `gitlab.v4.objects.Project.repository_tree(path, branch_name, all = True)`, but result was truncated, because of Gitlab pagination. 

From your docs, I thought, that `all = True` should work on any type of "request". I looked into the code and find out, that this isn't implemented.

Should this be implemented in your package or am I approaching this task in a wrong way?
There seems to be a problem with file names beginning with ".". This should be addressed.

issue_title: BatchNormalization failed when loading Caffe2 model converted from ONNX
issue_body:
I converted ONNX format to Caffe2 format. And then, when I load caffe2 model, I've got below error messages.

>
WARNING: Logging before InitGoogleLogging() is written to STDERR
E0115 13:50:47.453927  5523 operator.cc:130] Cannot find operator schema for BatchNormalization. Will skip schema checking.
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'caffe2::EnforceNotMet'
  what():  [enforce fail at operator.cc:190] op. Cannot create operator of type 'BatchNormalization' on the device 'CUDA'. Verify that implementation for the corresponding device exist. It might also happen if the binary is not linked with the operator implementation code. If Python frontend is used it might happen if dyndep.InitOpsLibrary call is missing. Operator def: input: "267" input: "3" input: "4" input: "5" input: "6" output: "269" name: "" type: "BatchNormalization" arg { name: "is_test" i: 1 } arg { name: "epsilon" f: 1e-05 } arg { name: "momentum" f: 0.9 } device_option { device_type: 1 }
Aborted (core dumped)
>

When I did same thing a few month ago, it worked fine. It converted BatchNormalization in ONNX to SpatialBN in Caffe2. However, the latest version of ONNX has this mis-conversion issue.

Originally reported by: **Sandor Toth (Bitbucket: [tothsa](https://bitbucket.org/tothsa), GitHub: Unknown)**

----------------------------------------

In Matlab the docstring ends at the first line without % symbol. For example:

```
#!Matlab

function testfun(a,b)
% this is the docstring
% this is still docstring

% this is code comment
return a+b
```
However using sphinxcontrib-matlabdomain with autodoc, the last comment is also added to the docstring.

----------------------------------------

issue_title: ZODB backend fails to remove triples
issue_body:
jus...@onebrightspace.com, 2009-09-01T09:29:08.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. Use ZODB backend.
2. Add a number of triples.
3. call remove((None, None, None))

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
Expected: All triples removed
Actual: Only a percentage of the triples are removed.

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
2.4.1

Please provide any additional information below.
The ZODB backend uses IOMemory.  The remove() method calls triples() to get
a list of triples to remove.  The triples method yields the results from a
{}.keys(); which is normally a list in Python, however on a BTree this
results in an iterable object.  Because the remove() method is then looping
around an iterable list and not a static list, the size of the list shrinks
as items are removed, resulting in missed triples and general odd behaviour.

Fix is to explicitly list() the tiples call in remove:

for triple, cg in list(self.triples(triple)):

This may be a rather large list, but the alternative is to list() the
keys() call in the triples method.  The triple method is likely to be used
a lot more than remove, so forcing it to list everything on the keys() call
will slow it down.

Comment 1 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 2 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 3 by eik...@gmail.com
No longer part of rdflib; at least for now.
